tools.name: get_system_info
tools.description: 获取系统信息，包括操作系统类型，cpu架构等。

tools.name: check_path_exists
tools.description:
检查路径是否存，如果存在，进一步判断这个路径是文件还是目录，返回路径类型。

Args:
    path: 要检查的路径

tools.name: get_directory_info
tools.description:
获取目录的详细信息，包括目录占磁盘空间，权限等信息。

Args:
    directory_path: 目录路径

tools.name: find_directory
tools.description:
在指定目录中查找子目录

Args:
    directory_path: 要搜索的目录路径
    name_pattern: 目录名模式（支持通配符，比如搜索名称中有trade的所有目录，可传参*trade*）

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description:
创建目录

Args:
    path: 要创建的目录路径
    exist_ok: 如果目录已存在是否忽略错误（默认False）
    parents: 是否创建父目录（默认True）

tools.name: delete_directory
tools.description:
删除目录

Args:
    path: 要删除的目录路径
    recursive: 是否递归删除子目录和文件（默认True）

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description:
列出目录内容

Args:
    directory_path: 要列出的目录路径
    sort_by: 排序方式（name/size/modified/type，默认name）
    reverse: 是否反向排序（默认False）
    filter_type: 过滤类型（file/directory，默认不过滤）

tools.name: rename_directory
tools.description:
重命名目录

Args:
    old_path: 原目录路径
    new_path: 新目录路径

tools.name: copy_directory
tools.description:
复制目录

Args:
    source_path: 源目录路径
    destination_path: 目标目录路径
    overwrite: 是否覆盖已存在的目录（默认False）

tools.name: move_directory
tools.description:
移动目录

Args:
    source_path: 源目录路径
    destination_path: 目标目录路径
    overwrite: 是否覆盖已存在的目录（默认False）

tools.name: get_directory_tree
tools.description:
获取目录树结构，相当于Linux中的tree命令，可指定最大深度。从而不用反复调用list_directory方法。

Args:
    directory_path: 目录路径
    max_depth: 最大深度（默认10）

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description:
获取文件信息

Args:
    file_path: 文件路径

tools.name: create_file
tools.description:
创建文件

Args:
    path: 文件路径
    content: 文件内容（可选）
    encoding: 文件编码（默认utf-8）
    exist_ok: 如果文件已存在是否忽略错误（默认False）

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description:
删除文件

Args:
    file_path: 文件路径

tools.name: rename_file
tools.description:
重命名文件

Args:
    old_path: 原文件路径
    new_path: 新文件路径

tools.name: copy_file
tools.description:
复制文件

Args:
    source_path: 源文件路径
    destination_path: 目标文件路径
    overwrite: 是否覆盖已存在的文件（默认False）

tools.name: move_file
tools.description:
移动文件

Args:
    source_path: 源文件路径
    destination_path: 目标文件路径
    overwrite: 是否覆盖已存在的文件（默认False）

tools.name: read_file
tools.description:
读取文件内容

Args:
    file_path: 文件路径
    start_line: 起始行号（可选）
    end_line: 结束行号（可选）
    encoding: 文件编码（默认utf-8）

tools.name: write_file
tools.description:
写入文件内容

Args:
    file_path: 文件路径
    content: 要写入的内容
    mode: 写入模式（overwrite/append/insert，默认overwrite）
    line_number: 插入模式下的行号（仅插入模式需要）
    encoding: 文件编码（默认utf-8）

tools.name: find_file
tools.description:
在指定目录中查找文件

Args:
    directory_path: 要搜索的目录路径
    name_pattern: 文件名模式（支持通配符）

findings.title: Parameter 'filter_type' in 'list_directory' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'filter_type'.

findings.title: Parameter 'content' in 'create_file' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'content'.

findings.title: Parameter 'start_line' in 'read_file' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'start_line'.

findings.title: Parameter 'end_line' in 'read_file' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'end_line'.

findings.title: Parameter 'line_number' in 'write_file' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'line_number'.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'create_file' conflicts with a high-value built-in name
findings.description: Tool 'create_file' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'delete_file' conflicts with a high-value built-in name
findings.description: Tool 'delete_file' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.description: Tool 'read_file' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'write_file' conflicts with a high-value built-in name
findings.description: Tool 'write_file' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: Token Exposure — internal IP address in 'get_system_info'
findings.description: Tool response contains internal IP address.
findings.evidence:
============================================================
SYSTEM INFORMATION
============================================================
Timestamp: 2026-07-05T20:26:08.696012
Python Version: 3.13.12 (main, Mar 24 2026, 22:56:40) [MSC v.1944 64 bit (AMD64)]
Python Implementation: CPython
Operating System: Windows 11
Architecture: AMD64
Platform: Windows-11-10.0.26200-SP0
Hostname: DESKTOP-TFV5V
findings.remediation: Strip sensitive data from tool responses. Never return raw environment variables, credentials, or internal paths.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 19 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Protocol crash — JSON-RPC batch array caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a JSON-RPC 2.0 batch (5 requests as an array) caused the server to crash or disconnect. MCP does not define batch semantics; servers must reject arrays gracefully.
findings.evidence: Server disconnected after batch. Pre-crash response: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
findings.remediation: Validate input type at the JSON-RPC layer: if the parsed value is a list, return -32600 (Invalid Request) immediately without further processing.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: Server accepts requests without jsonrpc field
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request missing the required 'jsonrpc' field. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, this field is mandatory and must equal '2.0'.
findings.remediation: Validate that the 'jsonrpc' field is present and equals '2.0' before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for non-conformant messages.

findings.title: Server accepts array request ID — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request with an array as the request ID. JSON-RPC 2.0 requires the id to be a string, number, or null.
findings.remediation: Validate that the request 'id' is a string, number, or null. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for array or object IDs.
